acceptance_targets: [ch01_F001] PASS if, by or before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:120, the text explicitly names Eli's hidden transfer and one concrete consequence (mortgage or tuition deadline), and each subsequent scene beat increases departure risk. FAIL if the first explicit articulation of the transfer still appears only at or after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:129.

[ch01_F002] PASS if the revised climax contains at least one on-page Nora communication delivering a concrete demand (deadline, amount, or action) and Eli performs an immediate consequential response (spoken lie, explicit refusal, or manipulative delay) before the lane commitment. FAIL if the scene remains hypothetical-only (e.g., 'he could still answer') plus missed calls.

[ch01_F003] PASS if, in the passage anchored by snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:150, Eli speaks at least one non-logistical truth fragment about not being okay or not going straight home, and June answers that fragment directly before leaving. FAIL if the scene resolves primarily through joke rhythm and affectionate tag lines.

[ch01_F004] PASS if each of the four cited speaker blocks includes at least one line whose diction and rhythm are character-specific enough to identify the speaker without dialogue tags. FAIL if removing tags still leaves two or more of these voices sounding interchangeable in the cited spans.

[ch01_F005] PASS if at least three of the six cited passages are rewritten to include a present decision, action consequence, or character inference instead of stacked noun inventory, and no more than two long inventory-stack constructions remain among those six targets. FAIL otherwise.

[ch01_F006] PASS if the revised span between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:57 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:83 contains at least three distinct paragraphs with explicit Eli interiority and no run longer than six consecutive paragraphs without interior access. FAIL if a 10+ paragraph external-only run remains in that span.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, by or before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:120, the text explicitly names Eli's hidden transfer and one concrete consequence (mortgage or tuition deadline), and each subsequent scene beat increases departure risk. FAIL if the first explicit articulation of the transfer still appears only at or after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:129.
findings.problem: The chapter delays its governing rupture too long. Extended tertiary move-in vignettes dominate the opening, while Eli's core transgression (the $14,000 transfer and intention not to return) is withheld until late, reducing early causal pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or intercut the vignette-heavy passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:45, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:57 with earlier intrusion of Eli's concealed financial action introduced before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:129. Preserve move-in texture, but tie each beat to the impending departure choice.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised climax contains at least one on-page Nora communication delivering a concrete demand (deadline, amount, or action) and Eli performs an immediate consequential response (spoken lie, explicit refusal, or manipulative delay) before the lane commitment. FAIL if the scene remains hypothetical-only (e.g., 'he could still answer') plus missed calls.
findings.problem: Sanitization-by-deferral at the climax: the marital rupture is handled as a hypothetical confession plus ignored calls, so summary replaces scene at peak pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the decision block beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:222 and running through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:234 so Nora's pressure lands on-page in real time (answered call, voicemail playback, or text exchange with immediate response), while keeping the westbound choice.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the passage anchored by snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:150, Eli speaks at least one non-logistical truth fragment about not being okay or not going straight home, and June answers that fragment directly before leaving. FAIL if the scene resolves primarily through joke rhythm and affectionate tag lines.
findings.problem: Timidity in the father-daughter break point: after a near-confession, the scene quickly retreats to protective banter and nickname mercy, softening immediate emotional cost before Eli lies.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the goodbye exchange around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:123 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:150 so Eli leaks a partial truth under pressure and June's response materially shifts the power of the exchange before she exits.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each of the four cited speaker blocks includes at least one line whose diction and rhythm are character-specific enough to identify the speaker without dialogue tags. FAIL if removing tags still leaves two or more of these voices sounding interchangeable in the cited spans.
findings.problem: Idiolect separation weakens across supporting speakers. Volunteer, Sloane, Tasha, and June often share a similar polished, ironic cadence, so register contrast flattens.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework spoken lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:57, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:95 to sharpen role-specific syntax and lexicon (procedural for staff, obsessive taxonomy for Sloane, adaptive collegiate rhythm for June).

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least three of the six cited passages are rewritten to include a present decision, action consequence, or character inference instead of stacked noun inventory, and no more than two long inventory-stack constructions remain among those six targets. FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: A recurring inventory-list construction appears more than twice and starts to read as mechanical style-bible literalism (objects/logistics/sensory piles) rather than evolving sentence design.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the inventory-heavy sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:67, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:158, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:166, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:180, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:238 by converting at least half into action-weighted or inference-bearing beats while preserving only the most strategic list accumulation.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:57 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:83 contains at least three distinct paragraphs with explicit Eli interiority and no run longer than six consecutive paragraphs without interior access. FAIL if a 10+ paragraph external-only run remains in that span.
findings.problem: Interiority drought: the stretch from the RA interruption through Nora's early text run contains 10+ consecutive paragraphs with little or no focalized internal experience, making the chapter briefly read like external staging.
findings.rewrite_direction: Within the block anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:57 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:83, add Eli's thought/sensation/self-assessment at key pressure moments (RA timing warning, med-form text, trash-night ping) without breaking scene momentum.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into morally mixed behavior, bodily consequence, and economic detail when they are causally active. In edge cases, choose the more concrete and uncomfortable rendering unless it violates factual continuity; if two renderings are equally plausible, prefer the one that increases character accountability rather than reader reassurance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene contains pain, sex, addiction, debt panic, or humiliation, abstract phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with embodied specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, legal precision, or deliberate emotional distance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter architectures across the book: confrontation chapters, document-information turns, labor-process chapters, transit interiority chapters, and quiet relational chapters. Consecutive chapters must differ in scene density or dialogue-to-interiority ratio.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary American literary realism: precise, unsentimental, image-aware, and concrete under stress.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third-person anchored to Eli, with no unmarked POV drift.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Braided cadence: reflective medium-length sentences interrupted by blunt short lines at impact moments.
